If we hope to spend eternity praising God, it is fitting that we should spend much of our time here doing this work. He never said to anyone, “Seek me in vain.” David’s prayers helped silence his fears; many besides him have looked to the Lord through faith and prayer, and it has wonderfully revived and comforted them. When we look to the world, we are perplexed and at a loss. But our whole salvation depends on looking to Christ, and all things needed for that also do so. This poor man, whom no one respected or cared for, was yet welcome to the throne of grace; the Lord heard him and saved him from all his troubles. The holy angels minister to the saints and stand for them against the powers of darkness. All glory be to the Lord of the angels. By taste and sight we both discover things and enjoy them; taste and see God’s goodness, notice it, and take comfort in it. He makes all who trust in him truly blessed. As for the things of the other world, they will have enough grace for spiritual life. And as for this life, they will have what is necessary from God's hand. Paul had everything and abounded because he was content. Those who trust in themselves and think their own efforts are enough will lack; but those who trust in the Lord will be fed. Those will not lack who quietly work and mind their own business.

Let young people begin life by learning to fear the Lord, if they desire true comfort here and eternal happiness hereafter. Those will be most happy who start serving such a good Master as soon as possible. Everyone aims to be happy. Surely this must look beyond the present world; for man’s life on earth consists of few days, and those full of trouble. What person would see the goodness of that place where all bliss is perfect? Alas! Few have this in their thoughts. That religion promises best which creates watchfulness over the heart and tongue. It is not enough not to do harm; we must study to be useful and live purposefully; we must seek peace and pursue it, being willing to deny ourselves much for peace’s sake. It is the constant practice of true believers when in distress to cry out to God, and their constant comfort is that he hears them. The righteous are humbled for sin and low in their own eyes. Nothing is more needed for true godliness than a contrite heart, broken off from every self-confidence. In this soil every grace will flourish, and nothing can encourage such a person but the free, rich grace of the gospel of Jesus Christ. The righteous are taken under the special protection of the Lord, yet they have their share of troubles in this world, and there are those who hate them. Both from the mercy of Heaven and the malice of hell, the afflictions of the righteous must be many. But whatever troubles befall them will not hurt their souls, for God keeps them from sinning in trouble. No one is desolate except he whom God has forsaken.
